summary_text: **Findings** - Current lock is PID-file based and race-prone under launchd restart loops. It relies on `os.kill(old_pid, 0)` and overwriting [scheduler_health.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler_health.py#L167), which is vulnerable to stale/reused PID and concurrent starts. - launchd is configured with `KeepAlive=true` and `ThrottleInterval=30` [com.cursorvers.x-auto-scheduler.plist](/Users/masayuki/Library/LaunchAgents/com.cursorvers.x-auto-scheduler.plist#L7), so any false lock conflict can become repeated restart churn. - There is already correct inter-process locking style in the codebase via `fcntl.flock` in `_post_due_jobs_lock` [scheduler_health.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler_health.py#L48); scheduler startup lock should use the same model. - Health checks already detect duplicate-start log lines [x-auto-health.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/eval/x-auto-health.py#L292), but startup lock correctness should not depend on log detection. **Recommended implementation (minimal-risk)** - Keep the public entrypoint name `_acquire_pid_lock()` to avoid call-site churn, but change internals to process-lifetime `flock` on a dedicated lock file (for example `.scheduler.run.lock`). - In `_acquire_pid_lock()`: - Open lock file once, store FD in module-global. - Acquire `LOCK_EX | LOCK_NB`; on `BlockingIOError`, emit alert and `SystemExit(1)` (same behavior as today, safer primitive). - Keep writing `.scheduler.pid` as informational metadata only (for tooling/backward compatibility), not as exclusivity mechanism. - Register `atexit` + `SIGTERM` cleanup to release lock and close FD. - Do not change launchd plist in this migration. Keep `KeepAlive`/`ThrottleInterval` unchanged until lock behavior is proven stable. - After one stable cycle, remove old `os.kill(old_pid, 0)` gating branch entirely. **Code changes** - Primary file: [scheduler_health.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler_health.py) - Add constants: lock-file path and global lock FD. - Refactor `_acquire_pid_lock()` to acquire `flock` first, then write PID metadata. - Keep current duplicate-start alert behavior; just trigger it from lock-conflict path. **Tests to add** - In [test_scheduler_r11_r12.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_scheduler_r11_r12.py) or a new `test_scheduler_health_lock.py`: - Lock acquisition succeeds and PID metadata is written. - Conflict path (`flock` raises `BlockingIOError`) exits with code 1 and emits duplicate-start alert. - Cleanup path releases lock on simulated `SIGTERM`. - Existing stale PID file does not block startup when lock is free. **Health-check updates** - Keep current collision scan in [x-auto-health.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/eval/x-auto-health.py#L292). - Add one consistency check: if `.scheduler.pid` exists and heartbeat exists, warn on mismatch between PID file and heartbeat PID (diagnostic only, non-blocking). This gives a low-risk migration: same operational behavior, safer exclusivity primitive, no launchd contract changes in phase 1.
